sections.short-biography: Warned Yu Jin of the risk of flooding but was ignored.

sections.literary-appearances: Romance of the Three Kingdoms: 74Sanguozhi: Wei 18
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Cheng He was a subordinate to Pang De around the year 219. When Guan Yu was closing in on their forces, Pang De told Cheng He, “I have heard that good generals do not cower in death just to escape and that fierce warriors do not sully their integrity just to live! Today is the day I will die!” It is unknown what happened to Cheng He thereafter.

In the novel, Cheng He warned the commanding general, Yu Jin, that their camp could be flooded by the nearby rivers, but Yu Jin ignored him. When the rivers indeed flooded the camp, Cheng He joined Pang De in holding out against Guan Yu. Cheng He bravely fought against the Shu forces, but he was killed by an arrow from Guan Yu.

full_text:
Cheng He 成何
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cheng He (成何). Warned Yu Jin of the risk of flooding but was ignored. Cheng He was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom. Cheng He lived during the Later Han Dynasty period of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Cheng He was a subordinate to Pang De around the year 219. When Guan Yu was closing in on their forces, Pang De told Cheng He, “I have heard that good generals do not cower in death just to escape and that fierce warriors do not sully their integrity just to live! Today is the day I will die!” It is unknown what happened to Cheng He thereafter.
In the novel, Cheng He warned the commanding general, Yu Jin, that their camp could be flooded by the nearby rivers, but Yu Jin ignored him. When the rivers indeed flooded the camp, Cheng He joined Pang De in holding out against Guan Yu. Cheng He bravely fought against the Shu forces, but he was killed by an arrow from Guan Yu.
